clusters.title: Taylor uses own televised address to criticise PM’s prime-time speech - video
clusters.neutral_headline: Angus Taylor Criticizes Prime Minister's National Address

items.title: Taylor uses own televised address to criticise PM’s prime-time speech - video
items.standfirst: Federal opposition leader Angus Taylor criticises the prime minister’s national address made on Wednesday night and says it failed to provide clarity or detail on the fuel crisis caused by the war in Iran. Taylor accuses the government of fuelling confusion and not acting with urgency, adding Australians 'received neither' answers nor detail Continue reading...
items.content: Federal opposition leader Angus Taylor criticises the prime minister’s national address made on Wednesday night and says it failed to provide clarity or detail on the fuel crisis caused by the war in Iran. Taylor accuses the government of fuelling confusion and not acting with urgency, adding Australians 'received neither' answers nor detail Continue reading...

items.title: Angus Taylor says 'put Australia first' during national address
items.standfirst: Angus Taylor has issued a return serve to the prime minister's national address by urging people to "put Australia first" while accusing the government of providing a lack of clarity about the ongoing fuel crisis.
items.content: Angus Taylor has issued a return serve to the prime minister's national address by urging people to "put Australia first" while accusing the government of providing a lack of clarity about the ongoing fuel crisis.

items.title: ‘Dig and drill’: Angus Taylor says Australia should fast-track mining and coal projects amid fuel crisis
items.standfirst: Opposition leader uses televised address to suggest Anthony Albanese’s televised address could have been a social media postGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastAngus Taylor says the global energy shock is proof that Australia should fast-track mining exploration and coal and gas project construction, as part of efforts to shore up fuel supply for future crises.Addressing Australians in a television broadcast on Thursday night, the opposition leader accused Labor of mishandling the disruption caused by the war in Iran and challenged Anthony Albanese to explain plans for emergency supply of petrol and diesel. Continue reading...
items.content: Opposition leader uses televised address to suggest Anthony Albanese’s televised address could have been a social media postGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastAngus Taylor says the global energy shock is proof that Australia should fast-track mining exploration and coal and gas project construction, as part of efforts to shore up fuel supply for future crises.Addressing Australians in a television broadcast on Thursday night, the opposition leader accused Labor of mishandling the disruption caused by the war in Iran and challenged Anthony Albanese to explain plans for emergency supply of petrol and diesel. Continue reading...

items.title: Anthony Albanese defends address to nation as bid to counter misinformation – video
items.standfirst: Australian prime minister, Anthony Albanese, defends his rare national address on Wednesday evening, delivered amid rising fuel supply concerns linked to the Middle East conflict. He says the address aimed to reassure Australians and counter growing misinformation, as social media disrupts access to consistent and reliable informationKeep calm, but here comes the pain. Albanese’s address presents Australians with a mixed messageAnthony Albanese addresses the nation on the Middle East crisis – read the speech in full Continue reading...
items.content: Australian prime minister, Anthony Albanese, defends his rare national address on Wednesday evening, delivered amid rising fuel supply concerns linked to the Middle East conflict. items.title: Keep calm, but here comes the pain. Albanese’s address presents Australians with a mixed message
items.standfirst: The prime minister’s decision to speak to the country could undermine what he was trying to convey, that everything is in hand and there is no need to panicGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastWhen news that Anthony Albanese was planning an address to the nation spread around Canberra on Wednesday morning, memories of those deep, dark days during the worst of the pandemic came rushing back.Amid fuel shortages and growing nervousness, a prime-time television address from the country’s leader was bound to remind anxious households of Covid-19 lockdowns and the chaos of the global financial crisis. Continue reading...
items.content: The prime minister’s decision to speak to the country could undermine what he was trying to convey, that everything is in hand and there is no need to panicGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastWhen news that Anthony Albanese was planning an address to the nation spread around Canberra on Wednesday morning, memories of those deep, dark days during the worst of the pandemic came rushing back.Amid fuel shortages and growing nervousness, a prime-time television address from the country’s leader was bound to remind anxious households of Covid-19 lockdowns and the chaos of the global financial crisis.
